basicDetails.schemeName: Award of Cash Incentive to the Toppers at Regional Level and Students Scoring Centum in the Subjects Involving Theory in Secondary School Leaving Certificate/Secondary School Certificate/Intermediate/Higher Secondary Public Examinations in Government Schools in the Union Territory of Puducherry

schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme aims to award cash incentives to encourage students in their education by recognizing top three regional rank holders and those scoring centum in theory subjects in Secondary School Leaving Certificate/Secondary School Certificate/Intermediate/Higher Secondary Exams in Govt. Schools.
schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The scheme "Award of Cash Incentive to the Toppers at Regional Level and Students Scoring Centum in the Subjects Involving Theory in Secondary School Leaving Certificate/Secondary School Certificate/Intermediate/Higher Secondary Public Examinations in Government Schools in the Union Territory of Puducherry" was launched by the Department of School Education, Puducherry. The quantum of Award ranges from ₹10,000/- up to ₹20,000/- for top ranks depending on the position, and ₹5,000/- per subject for centum scores.
schemeContent.benefits_md: - For students securing top three ranks at the regional level in Secondary School Leaving Certificate/Secondary School Certificate/Intermediate/Higher Secondary Public Examinations in Government Schools: 1st Rank: ₹20,000/-; 2nd Rank: ₹15,000/-; 3rd Rank: ₹10,000/-. - For students scoring centum in the subjects involving theory in Secondary School Leaving Certificate/Higher Secondary Course Examinations: ₹5,000/- per subject. - **Mode of Disbursement:** The award will be given through Direct Benefit Transfer or by Demand Draft.- **Frequency:** The award is disbursed to the eligible beneficiaries every year.- **Tie-Breaker Condition:** In the event of more than one student getting equal marks, the concerned full prize amount will be distributed to each of them in full. This distribution includes the marks secured by way of revaluation at a later date as prescribed by the concerned Board.

children.text: The scheme "Award of Cash Incentive to the Toppers at Regional Level and Students Scoring Centum in the Subjects Involving Theory in Secondary School Leaving Certificate/Secondary School Certificate/Intermediate/Higher Secondary Public Examinations in Government Schools in the Union Territory of Puducherry" was launched by the Department of School Education, Puducherry. The quantum of Award ranges from ₹10,000/- up to ₹20,000/- for top ranks depending on the position, and ₹5,000/- per subject for centum scores.

For students securing top three ranks at the regional level in Secondary School Leaving Certificate/Secondary School Certificate/Intermediate/Higher Secondary Public Examinations in Government Schools: 1st Rank: ₹20,000/-; 2nd Rank: ₹15,000/-; 3rd Rank: ₹10,000/-.
For students scoring centum in the subjects involving theory in Secondary School Leaving Certificate/Higher Secondary Course Examinations: ₹5,000/- per subject.
Mode of Disbursement:
The award will be given through Direct Benefit Transfer or by Demand Draft.
Frequency:
The award is disbursed to the eligible beneficiaries every year.
Tie-Breaker Condition:

In the event of more than one student getting equal marks, the concerned full prize amount will be distributed to each of them in full. This distribution includes the marks secured by way of revaluation at a later date as prescribed by the concerned Board.
*The process is primarily an internal selection methodology conducted by the administering department based on examination results.
Step 1:
Preparation of the List of Eligible Students

The Directorate of School Education, Puducherry, will prepare the initial list of eligible students for the Award of cash incentive based on the total marks obtained in the respective Public Examination.
This preparation utilizes the result analysis published by the concerned Board (Secondary School Leaving Certificate/Secondary School Certificate/Intermediate/Higher Secondary Public Examinations).
Step 2:
Final Determination in Case of Disputes
If any doubts or disputes arise concerning the selection or eligibility during the implementation of the Scheme, the matter shall be referred to the Secretary to Government (Education).
The decision made by the Secretary to Government (Education) regarding the interpretation or clarification of any provision shall be considered final.
Disbursement of the Award
Once the list of eligible beneficiaries is finalized annually, the corresponding cash award will be disbursed through either Direct Benefit Transfer or by Demand Draft.
If multiple students secure equal marks, the full prize amount corresponding to the rank achieved will be distributed in full to each student, even if the marks were secured via revaluation.

applicationProcess.process_md: ****The process is primarily an internal selection methodology conducted by the administering department based on examination results.*** ##### **Step 1:****** ***Preparation of the List of Eligible Students*** - The Directorate of School Education, Puducherry, will prepare the initial list of eligible students for the Award of cash incentive based on the total marks obtained in the respective Public Examination. - This preparation utilizes the result analysis published by the concerned Board (Secondary School Leaving Certificate/Secondary School Certificate/Intermediate/Higher Secondary Public Examinations). ##### **Step 2:****** ***Final Determination in Case of Disputes*** - If any doubts or disputes arise concerning the selection or eligibility during the implementation of the Scheme, the matter shall be referred to the Secretary to Government (Education). - The decision made by the Secretary to Government (Education) regarding the interpretation or clarification of any provision shall be considered final. ##### ***Disbursement of the Award*** - Once the list of eligible beneficiaries is finalized annually, the corresponding cash award will be disbursed through either Direct Benefit Transfer or by Demand Draft. - If multiple students secure equal marks, the full prize amount corresponding to the rank achieved will be distributed in full to each student, even if the marks were secured via revaluation.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: - The applicant must be a citizen of India. - The applicant must be a resident of the Union territory of Puducherry, by virtue of birth or continuous residence of not less than five years. - The applicant must be a student appearing in Secondary School Leaving Certificate/Secondary School Certificate/Intermediate/Higher Secondary Public Examinations in Government Schools. - The applicant must have passed the qualifying examination in the first attempt. - The applicant's marks from consecutive attempts must not be taken into account.

children.text: The applicant must be a citizen of India.
The applicant must be a resident of the Union territory of Puducherry, by virtue of birth or continuous residence of not less than five years.
The applicant must be a student appearing in Secondary School Leaving Certificate/Secondary School Certificate/Intermediate/Higher Secondary Public Examinations in Government Schools.
The applicant must have passed the qualifying examination in the first attempt.
The applicant's marks from consecutive attempts must not be taken into account.
